frame_analyses.description: First instrument enters the field and begins orienting the needle and suture near the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Initial setup controlled; brief delay before needle is fully optimized for the first pass.

frame_analyses.description: Needle driven through the first marked side of the drain with the opposite instrument providing countertraction.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Entry angle acceptable; bite appears close to the intended mark.

frame_analyses.description: Suture drawn through both sides of the Penrose; limbs separated across the drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Placement reasonably close to both side marks; exact mm measurement low confidence from this camera angle.

frame_analyses.description: A double wrap is formed and the first throw is cinched down at the drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Surgeon's knot with right instrument likely leading; several regrasps needed before it lays flat.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw being formed over the initial knot stack with limbs crossed in the opposite direction.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Probable hand switch; knot security improves but tail management is slow.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw placed and tightened over the prior throws.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Alternation appears preserved; exact lead hand partially obscured by viewing angle.

frame_analyses.description: Final snugging and additional reinforcing manipulation continue after the main knot stack is established.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Construct remains intact and centered; extra regrasping prolongs the task.

frame_analyses.description: Cutting instrument enters from the left; tail division begins.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instrument exchange late and deliberate; first cut controlled but not efficient.

frame_analyses.description: Both suture ends appear cut; instruments begin to leave the field.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: No drain avulsion or obvious knot unraveling at completion.

knot_assessments.notes: Double wrap visible before cinching; right-sided lead most likely interpretation.
Second throw reverses crossing direction; likely uses opposite hand. Moderate confidence — instruments partially obscure the loop.
Third single throw with return to original lead hand. Reinforcing manipulation likely follows the primary third throw.

technique_summary: V11 scored at 198.3s / 398.2 FLS. Needle passage reasonably close to target marks; drain remained fixed with no avulsion or obvious knot failure and the final stack appeared secure with adequately approximated slit. Performance dominated by efficiency losses — repeated regrasping, prolonged tail management, and likely extra reinforcing motion after the required three throws. Second + third throws together consumed ~133s (about two-thirds of the whole task).
improvement_suggestions: Shorten the working loop and pre-position the standing limb after needle passage so the first throw can begin sooner.
Apply more direct symmetric opposing tension after each throw to square the knot earlier and reduce repeated regrasping.
Prepare the cutter exchange earlier and align both tails more efficiently to shorten the final cutting phase.
strengths: Needle bites appear reasonably close to the side marks without tearing or avulsing the drain.
First throw looks like a true double wrap and the final knot remains intact through completion and cutting.
comparison_notes: Paired with score_claude_V11_001 — see memory/comparisons/V11_teacher_comparison_20260407.json. ΔFLS 1.2 (strongest agreement in the V-series so far, even tighter than V14). Teachers converge on total time (195 vs 198.3) but diverge heavily on phase boundaries: Claude puts bulk of time in suture_placement (50s) and suture_cut (50s), GPT-4o puts bulk in second_throw (50s) and third_throw (83s).
